title: user — trait composition
description: Three owned traits. `BelongsToUser` pairs a domain model with a User FK + auto-fills `user_id` on save. `HasProfile` gives services the accessor chain into the Profile satellite without wide model exposure. `IsUserOwnable` is a marker interface + tiny helper that the `.own` scope resolver consults when narrowing queries by `user_id = current_user_id`.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a User FK. Adds the `user()` belongsTo relation, the global scope filter used by the base repository, and a `booted()` hook that fills `user_id = auth()->id()` on `creating` when the field is null AND the current request has an authenticated User principal. Order-sensitive when composed with `BelongsToTenant` — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context in this trait's `booted()` hook.
usedBy: sports::Athlete (via user_id nullable — minors have no login)

owned.description: Exposes `profile()` hasOne + a set of accessors that pull from the Profile satellite (`full_name`, `display_name`, `avatar_url`, `locale`, `timezone`, `dob`) with fallbacks to identity + tenant defaults. Composed by the User model. Callers that only need to read profile fields go through the accessors on User rather than joining Profile directly — that keeps the PII cluster behind one redactor boundary.

Marker interface + one helper: `scopeOwnedBy(User $user)` on the composing model, applying `WHERE user_id = $user->id`. Consumed by the `ScopeSuffixResolver` when a caller's permission carries the `.own` suffix. The trait doesn't need `booted()` — it's a query-time helper; the actual filter is applied by the base repository, not the model.

framework.reason: User receives lifecycle notifications via mail + in_app channels.
Delegates to identity->email_verified_at — User row is verified when its Identity is verified.

audit.reason: User + Profile mutations write to the `audits` table (compliance signal).

activity.reason: User lifecycle events also feed the product-facing activity feed.

user.notes: Order matters. BelongsToTenant MUST precede HasProfile so accessors observe the tenant scope. HasApiTokens comes before Notifiable because Sanctum registers its own auth guard on boot.

profile.notes: PII cluster — Auditable writes every mutation to the compliance audit trail. NO HasActivityLog because Profile updates are already logged on the User's activity stream via the ProfileUpdated event.

tenant_member.notes: Pivot rows are auditable + activity-logged because role changes are compliance-relevant. SoftDeletes so we can restore an accidentally-removed membership within the retention window.
